Items.Title: 我的个人资料网站现在是一个终端 | My profile website is now a terminal
Items.Description:
当我年轻的时候，我曾经认为我的个人资料网站会是一个非常酷的、功能齐全的网站，具有闪亮的色彩和动画； 采用尖端的前端技术构建...
事实证明，年龄越大，我越喜欢简单的终端。 没有 UI，只有文本和命令。

上次我更新我的个人资料网站时，它看起来像这样：

它已经很简约了，对吧？ 但还不够。 不，我的个人资料网站只是一个终端：

让我们看看这是怎么可能的。

务实的方法

几天前，我在脑海中形成了这个想法，发现了这个很酷的库： xterms 。 它已被许多应用程序使用，VS Code 就是其中之一。 我决定试一试，看看它有多复杂，所以我前往文档并开始将代码添加到我的网站。 如您所见，文档非常好，它们肯定是从 TS 文档自动生成的，但这很好，因为这意味着代码本身有很好的文档记录。

在编码之前，我设置了一些要求：

  • 我不想使用npm模块。 我希望我的网站源代码简单明了
  • 我想使用所有（相关）浏览器都支持的javascript 模块
  • 终端命令应该是抽象的，以便我可以随意删除或添加命令，只需进行一些更改

那么，如何在不使用npm的情况下安装xtermjs ？ 解决方案很简单，我托管文件。 我使用此命令从 npm 包中提取了文件

npm v xterm dist.tarball | xargs curl | tar -xz

并将package/lib/xterm.js到app/

要使用 javascript 模块，我只需要将main.js文件作为模块导入

<script type= "module" src= "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/protiumx/blog/main/content/posts/006/app/main.js" ></script>

终端命令

虽然不使用typescript让我们说终端命令实现以下​​接口

interface Command { id : string ; description : string ; usage : string ; args : number ; run : ( terminal : Terminal ) => Promise < void > ; }

然后我们需要一个命令运行器来解析用户输入

interface CommandRunner { ( term : Terminal , userInput : string ) => Promise < boolean > ; }

如果未找到命令，则运行器将返回false 。 现在让我们定义 1 个命令： 

 const lsCommand = { id : " ls " , description : ' list files ' , usage : ' [usage]: ls filename ' args : 0 , async run ( term , args ) { for ( const file of files ) { term . write ( file . name + ' 		 ' ); } }, };

现在我们塑造了command ，我们可以考虑处理用户输入。

终端基本功能

终端应支持：

  • 它应该显示一个prompt

  • 终端还应处理常见错误：

    • 找不到相关命令
    • 带有错误参数的命令

    考虑到这一点，我们可以开始处理用户输入。

    这意味着我们需要跟踪用户输入并将每个键添加为字符。 当用户按下enter键时，我们应该评估到目前为止的输入。 相当直截了当

    let userInput = '' ; if ( ev . keyCode == 13 ) { await runCommand ( term , userInput ); userInput = '' ; prompt ( term ); } else { term . write ( key ); userInput += key ; }

    注意： xterm不呈现用户输入，所以我们需要在有意义的时候进行（不是输入，不是箭头键等）

    处理清屏可以实现为

    if ( ev . ctrlKey && ev . key === ' l ' ) { term . clear (); return ; }

    和SIGINT

     if ( ev . ctrlKey && ev . key === ' c ' ) { prompt ( term ); userInput = '' ; return ; }

    至此，我们有一个非常基本的工作终端，所以让我们添加更多命令

    基本命令

    最知名的命令是什么？ 对于我的终端，我希望能够使用cat 、 ls 、 rm 、 exit 。 但请记住，这个终端实际上​​是我的个人资料网站，所以它们在这种情况下应该是有意义的。 所以我决定终端应该有一个文件系统，其中文件的形状像

    interface File { name : string ; content : string ; }

    例子

    const files = [{ name : " about.md " , content : " once upon a time " }];

    考虑到这一点， cat将打印文件内容， ls将打印每个文件的名称， rm将从数组中删除文件。

    对于exit命令，我们可以从 javascript 中关闭窗口： window.close() 。

    走得更远

    我决定我想要一个名为blog.md的文件，它应该包含我最近的 5 篇文章。
    为了获取这些信息，我使用了hugo为我的博客生成的 RSS 提要 xml 文件。 我需要做的就是获取文件，解析xml文档并获取每个帖子的标题和链接：

     export async function fecthLastPosts () { const res = await fetch ( ' /blog/index.xml ' ); const text = await res . text (); const parser = new DOMParser (); const xmlDoc = parser . parseFromString ( text , " text/xml " ); const posts = xmlDoc . getElementsByTagName ( ' item ' ); const lastPosts = []; for ( let i = 0 ; i < 5 ; i ++ ) { const title = posts [ i ]. getElementsByTagName ( ' title ' )[ 0 ]. childNodes [ 0 ]. nodeValue ; const link = posts [ i ]. getElementsByTagName ( ' link ' )[ 0 ]. childNodes [ 0 ]. nodeValue ; lastPosts . push ( title + `
 ${ link } 
` ); } files [ 0 ]. content = lastPosts . join ( ' 
 ' ); }

    现在cat blog.md打印了我最近的 5 篇文章，并且感谢xterm的web link插件，每个链接都是可点击的。 噪音。
    但为什么要停在这里？ 每个hackerman终端都应该有一个whoami命令。 所以这个命令只会打印关于我自己的信息。

    此外，很酷的网络应用程序包含猫的照片，所以我决定编写一个randc命令来打开一张猫的随机照片。
    为此，我发现了这个惊人的休息 API

     { id : " randc " , description : ' get a random cat photo ' , args : 0 , async run ( term , args ) { term . writeln ( ' getting a cato... ' ); const res = await fetch ( ' https://cataas.com/cat?json=true ' ); if ( ! res . ok ) { term . writeln ( `[error] no catos today :( -- ${ res . statusText } ` )); } else { const { url } = await res . json (); term . writeln ( colorize ( TermColors . Green , ' opening cato... ' )); await sleep ( 1000 ); window . open ( ' https://cataas.com ' + url ); } }, },

    结果：

    我认为这应该用于profile terminal 。 我对它的简单性和我实现的命令非常满意。
    将来我可能会添加更多命令并实现streams ，只是为了好玩。

    您将向您的个人资料终端添加什么命令？

    其他文章：

    👽 👽

    Njiva Olaf { dev-it-out } - Njiva Olaf { dev-it-out } - May 16 5月16日



原文:

When I was younger I used to think that my profile website would be a really cool, a full featured website, with shiny colors and animations; built with a cutting edge frontend technology...
Turns out that the older I get the more I prefer a simple terminal. No UI, just text and commands.

The last time I updated my profile website, it looked like this:

It was already pretty minimalistic, right? But not enough. No my profile website is just a terminal:

Let's see how this was possible.

A pragmatic approach

A few days ago I was shaping this idea on my head and found this cool library: xterms. It's been used by a lot of apps, VS Code being among them. I decided to give it a try to see how complex could it be, so I headed to the docs and started adding the code to my website. As you can see the docs are pretty good, they are surely autogenerated from TS docs but this is good because it means the code itself is well documented.

Before coding I set a few requirements:

  • I don't want to use npm modules. I want my website source to be simple and minimal
  • I want to make use of javascript modules which are supported by all (relevant) browsers
  • The terminal commands should be abstract to allow me to remove or add commands at will with a few changes

Then, how do I install xtermjs without using npm? The solution is simple, I host the files. I extracted the files from the npm packages with this command

npm v xterm dist.tarball | xargs curl | tar -xz

and moved package/lib/xterm.js into app/

To use javascript modules, I just needed to import the main.js file as module

<script type="module" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/protiumx/blog/main/content/posts/006/app/main.js"></script>

Terminal Commands

Although not using typescript let's say that the terminal commands implement the following interface

interface Command { id: string; description: string; usage: string; args: number; run: (terminal: Terminal) => Promise<void>;
}

Then we need a command runner that will parse the user input

interface CommandRunner { (term: Terminal, userInput: string) => Promise<boolean>;
}

The runner will return false if a command was not found. Let's now define 1 command:

const lsCommand = { id: "ls", description: 'list files', usage: '[usage]: ls filename' args: 0, async run(term, args) { for (const file of files) { term.write(file.name + '		'); } },
};

Now that we shaped the command, we can think of handling user input.

Terminal basic functionality

The terminal should support:

  • It should show a prompt

  • ctrl + l: should clear the terminal

  • ctrl + c: should mean SIGINT

  • enter: should run a command from the current user input

The terminal should also handle common errors:

  • command not found
  • command with wrong arguments

With this in mind we can start handling the user input.

xterm provides a onKey event which receives a handler function ({ key, domEvent }) => void, so we receive an event per each key press done by the user. This means that we need to track the user input and add each key as a char. When the user presses enter we should evaluate the input we have so far. Pretty straigt forward

let userInput = '';
if (ev.keyCode == 13) { await runCommand(term, userInput); userInput = ''; prompt(term);
} else { term.write(key); userInput += key;
}

NOTE: xterm doesn't render the user input, so we need to do it when it makes sense (not enter, not an arrow key, etc)

Handling the clear-screen can be implemented as

if (ev.ctrlKey && ev.key === 'l') { term.clear(); return;
}

and the SIGINT

if (ev.ctrlKey && ev.key === 'c') { prompt(term); userInput = ''; return;
}

At this point we have a pretty basic working terminal, so let's add some more commands

Basic commands

What are the most known commands? For my terminal I want to be able to use cat, ls, rm, exit. But remember that this terminal is actually my profile website, so they should make sense in that context. So I decided the terminal should have a file system, where files are shaped like

interface File { name: string; content: string;
}

Example

const files = [{ name: "about.md", content: "once upon a time"}];

With this in mind, cat will print the file content, ls will print each file's name and rm will delete the file from the array.

For the exit command we can just close the window from javascript: window.close().

Going further

I have decided that I wanted to have a file named blog.md which should contain my last 5 posts.
To fetch this info, I used the RSS feed xml file generated by hugo for my blog. All I need to do is to fetch the file, parse the xml document and get the title and links of each post:

export async function fecthLastPosts() { const res = await fetch('/blog/index.xml'); const text = await res.text(); const parser = new DOMParser(); const xmlDoc = parser.parseFromString(text,"text/xml"); const posts = xmlDoc.getElementsByTagName('item'); const lastPosts = []; for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) { const title = posts[i].getElementsByTagName('title')[0].childNodes[0].nodeValue; const link = posts[i].getElementsByTagName('link')[0].childNodes[0].nodeValue; lastPosts.push(title + `
${link}
`); } files[0].content = lastPosts.join('
');
}

Now cat blog.md prints my last 5 posts, and thanks to the web link addon of xterm each link is clickeable. Noice.
But why stopping here? Every hackerman terminal should have a whoami command. So this command will just print information about my self.

Also, cool web apps contain photos of cats, so I decided to write a randc command what will open a rando photo of a cat.
For this I found this amazing rest API

 { id: "randc", description: 'get a random cat photo', args: 0, async run(term, args) { term.writeln('getting a cato...'); const res = await fetch('https://cataas.com/cat?json=true'); if (!res.ok) { term.writeln(`[error] no catos today :( -- ${res.statusText}`)); } else { const { url } = await res.json(); term.writeln(colorize(TermColors.Green, 'opening cato...')); await sleep(1000); window.open('https://cataas.com' + url); } }, },

The result:

I think this should do it for a profile terminal. I'm very satisfied with the simplicity of it and the commands I have implemented.
I'll problaly add more commands in the future and also implement streams , just for fun.

What command would you add to yout profile terminal?

Other articles:

👽

Njiva Olaf { dev-it-out } - May 16
Items.Author: protium

Items.Title: 你可能不知道的 7 个令人震惊的 HTML 技巧 | 7 Shocking HTML tips you probably don't know about
Items.Description:
这些是我学到的一些 HTML 技巧的集合，绝对会让你大吃一惊！

正如input标签具有电子邮件、文本和密码的属性一样，还有一个属性可以打开移动设备的摄像头以捕获图像。

这是通过capture属性完成的，该属性可以采用两个值：

  • 前置摄像头用户
  • 后置摄像头环境
<input type="file" capture="user" accept="image/*">

您可以将您的网站设置为在head标签的给定时间后刷新！

 <head> <meta http-equiv="refresh" content="10"> </head>

此代码段每 10 秒刷新一次网站

您可以使用 HTML spellcheck属性并将其设置为true以激活它。 使用lang属性指定要使用的语言

<input type="text" spellcheck="true" lang="en">

它是一个标准属性，大多数浏览器都支持

我想知道 dev.to 是否在他们的创建帖子编辑器上使用了这个属性...... :laugh:

您可以使用accept属性在input标签中指定允许用户上传的文件类型

<input type="file" accept=".jpeg,.png">

将translate属性设置为no会阻止翻译。 如果您不想翻译某个短语或单词，例如您的徽标、公司或品牌名称，这一点很重要。

 <p translate="no">Brand name</p>

这可以通过multiple属性来完成

<input type="file" multiple>

它适用于文件和电子邮件。 对于电子邮件，用逗号分隔每封电子邮件。

使用poster属性，您可以创建在视频下载时显示的图像，或者直到用户点击播放按钮。

如果这不包括在内，则将使用视频的第一帧。

 <video poster="picture.png"></video>

如果您希望在单击目标资源的链接时下载特定资源，请添加download属性

<a href="image.png" download>

这是一个包装。 一旦我了解更多，我一定会分享更多😄

在下面评论哪些对你来说是新的



原文:

These are a collection of some HTML tips I learned that would definitely blow your mind!

1. Capture attribute to open your device camera

Just as the input tag has attributes for email, text and password, there is also an attribute to open the camera of mobile devices to capture images.

This is done with the capture attribute which can take two values:

  • user for the front camera
  • environment for the back camera 
<input type="file" capture="user" accept="image/*">

2. Automatic website refresh

You can set your website to refresh after a given amount of time from the head tag!

<head>
    <meta http-equiv="refresh" content="10">
</head>

This snippet refreshes the website every 10 seconds

3. Activate spellcheck

You can use the HTML spellcheck attribute and set it to true to activate it. Specify the language to be used using the lang attribute

<input type="text" spellcheck="true" lang="en">

It is a standard attribute and is supported by most browsers

I wonder if dev.to uses this attribute on their create post editor... :laugh:

3. Specify file type to be uploaded

You can specify the file types users are permitted to upload in the input tag using the accept attribute

<input type="file" accept=".jpeg,.png">

4. Prevent translation

Setting the translate attribute to no, prevents translation. This is important in case where you don't want a phrase or word to be translated, for example your logo, company or brand name.

<p translate="no">Brand name</p>

5. Entering multiple items into the input tag

This can be done with the multiple attribute

<input type="file" multiple>

It works for both files and emails. For emails, separate each email by a comma.

6. Creating a poster(thumbnail) for your videos

With the poster attribute, you can create an image which is displayed while the video is downloading, or until the user hits the play button.

If this is not included, the first frame of the video will be used instead.

<video poster="picture.png"></video>

7. Automatically download on link click

If you want a particular resource to be downloaded when a link to the target resource is clicked, add the download attribute

<a href="image.png" download>

That's a wrap. I'm sure to share more once I learn more 😄

Comment below which ones were new to you
Items.Author: babib

Items.Title: Svelte 应用程序：使用 svelte/motion 创建 Loading Overlay 组件 | Svelte app: create Loading Overlay component using svelte/motion
Items.Description:
对于我的一个项目，我需要创建一个加载覆盖组件来隐藏应用程序，直到加载完成。 在这篇文章中，我想分享我是如何做到的。

首先，让我们勾勒出 HTML 和 CSS 标记。 这很简单。 我创建了一个块，将其固定放置，将其拉伸到全屏大小，并将其内容居中。 注意我们如何使用 svg 渐变来填充不同颜色的文本。

 <div class= "overlay" > <svg width= "115" height= "32" viewBox= "0 0 78 32" xmlns= "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" > <defs> <linearGradient id= "filler" > <stop offset= "50%" stop-color= "#ff006e" /> <stop offset= "50%" stop-color= "white" /> </linearGradient> </defs> <text x= "0" y= "24px" fill= "url(#filler)" > Loading... </text> </svg> </div> <style> .overlay { position : fixed ; top : 0 ; right : 0 ; bottom : 0 ; left : 0 ; z-index : 10001 ; display : flex ; align-items : center ; justify-content : center ; background : #3a86ff ; font-family : "Arial" , sans-serif ; font-size : 1.375rem ; } </style>

凉爽的。 我们完成了标记。 现在让我们继续动画。 为此，我们将使用标准 Svelte 工具。 使用svelte/motion的tweened ，我们创建了一个progress变量，并在 svg 渐变中使用它的值。

 const progress = tweened ( 0 , { easing : linear , duration : 3000 });
 <linearGradient id= "filler" > <stop offset= {`${$progress}%`} stop-color= "#ff006e" /> <stop offset= {`${$progress}%`} stop-color= "white" /> </linearGradient>

接下来，让我们创建我们将在组件挂载时调用的animate函数。 我们使这个函数递归，以便动画无限期地继续。 像任何其他递归函数一样，它必须有一个退出点。 因此，为了避免内存泄漏，我们确保函数的执行在组件实例被销毁时结束。

 let destroyed = false async function animate () { if ( destroyed ) { return } await width . set ( 100 ) await width . set ( 0 , { duration : 0 }) await animate () } onMount (() => { animate () }) onDestroy (() => { destroyed = true })

美好的。 我们的组件看起来已经不错了。 现在我们将通过两阶段改进生成的动画，在第一阶段我们用粉红色在白色文本上绘制，反之亦然，在第 2 阶段我们用白色在粉红色文本上绘制。 为此，我们将创建一个带有布尔值的writable存储，我们将使用它来指示动画的当前阶段。 每次调用animate函数时，我们都会将此值更改为相反的值。

 const isFirstStage = writable ( true ) async function animate () { if ( destroyed ) { return } await width . set ( 100 ) await width . set ( 0 , { duration : 0 }) isFirstStage . update (( value ) => ! value ) await animate () }
 <linearGradient id= "filler" > <stop offset= {`${$progress}%`} stop-color= {$isFirstStage ? ' #ff006e ' : ' white '} /> <stop offset= {`${$progress}%`} stop-color= {$isFirstStage ? ' white ' : ' #ff006e '} /> </linearGradient>

因此，我们完成了加载指示器组件本身。 现在让我们假设可以有几个组件使用我们的指标。 我们可以在它们中的每一个中渲染我们的叠加层，但让我们更好地改进我们的方法并创建允许我们渲染一次指示器并提供打开和关闭指示器的函数的功能。 为此，我们将创建另一个writable存储，其中将包含显示指标的请求。 只要商店中至少还有一个请求，就会呈现指示器本身。

 import { writable , derived } from " svelte/store " ; import { nanoid } from " nanoid " ; export const loadingOverlayQueue = writable ([]); export function showLoadingOverlay () { const newRequestId = nanoid (); loadingOverlayQueue . update (( currentValue ) => [... currentValue , newRequestId ]); return newRequestId ; } export function hideLoadingOverlay ( requestId ) { loadingOverlayQueue . update (( currentValue ) => currentValue . filter (( item ) => item !== requestId ) ); } export const isLoadingOverlayShown = derived ( loadingOverlayQueue , ( requests ) => requests . length > 0 );

现在，一切都完成了。 我们通过几个简单的步骤创建了一个原始的动画加载指示器。 源代码可以在我的 GitHub 上找到。 你喜欢和 svelte 一起工作吗？



原文:

For one of my projects, I needed to create a loading overlay component to hide the app until the loading is complete. In this article, I want to share how I did it.

First, let's sketch out the HTML and CSS markup. This is quite simple. I create a block, position it fixed, stretch it to full-screen size, and centre its content. Notice how we use the svg gradient to fill the text with different colours.

<div class="overlay"> <svg width="115" height="32" viewBox="0 0 78 32" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"> <defs> <linearGradient id="filler"> <stop offset="50%" stop-color="#ff006e" /> <stop offset="50%" stop-color="white" /> </linearGradient> </defs> <text x="0" y="24px" fill="url(#filler)">Loading...</text> </svg>
</div> <style> .overlay { position: fixed; top: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0; left: 0; z-index: 10001; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: #3a86ff; font-family: "Arial", sans-serif; font-size: 1.375rem; }
</style>

Cool. We are done with the markup. Now let's move on to animation. To do this, we will use standard Svelte tools. Using tweened from svelte/motion we create a progress variable and use its value in the svg gradient.

const progress = tweened(0, { easing: linear, duration: 3000 });

 <linearGradient id="filler"> <stop offset={`${$progress}%`} stop-color="#ff006e" /> <stop offset={`${$progress}%`} stop-color="white" /> </linearGradient>

Next, let's create animate function that we will call when the component mounts. We make this function recursive so that the animation continues indefinitely. Like any other recursive function, it must have an exit point. Therefore, in order to avoid memory leaks, we make sure that the execution of the function ends when the component instance is destroyed.

 let destroyed = false async function animate() { if (destroyed) { return } await width.set(100) await width.set(0, { duration: 0 }) await animate() } onMount(() => { animate() }) onDestroy(() => { destroyed = true })

Fine. Our component already looks good. Now we will improve the resulting animation by making it two-stage, where in the first stage we paint over the white text with pink, and vice versa, in stage number 2 we paint over the pink text with white. To do this, we will create a writable store with a boolean value that we will use to indicate the current stage of the animation. With each call of the animate function, we will change this value to the opposite.

 const isFirstStage = writable(true) async function animate() { if (destroyed) { return } await width.set(100) await width.set(0, { duration: 0 }) isFirstStage.update((value) => !value) await animate() }

<linearGradient id="filler"> <stop offset={`${$progress}%`} stop-color={$isFirstStage ? '#ff006e' : 'white'} /> <stop offset={`${$progress}%`} stop-color={$isFirstStage ? 'white' : '#ff006e'} />
</linearGradient>

So, we are done with the loading indicator component itself. Now let's imagine that there can be several components that use our indicator. We could render our overlay in each of them, but let's better refine our approach and create functionality that will allow us to render the indicator once and provide functions for turning the indicator on and off. To do this, we will create another writable store, which will contain requests to display the indicator. The indicator itself will be rendered as long as there is at least one request left in the store.

import { writable, derived } from "svelte/store";
import { nanoid } from "nanoid"; export const loadingOverlayQueue = writable([]); export function showLoadingOverlay() { const newRequestId = nanoid(); loadingOverlayQueue.update((currentValue) => [...currentValue, newRequestId]); return newRequestId;
} export function hideLoadingOverlay(requestId) { loadingOverlayQueue.update((currentValue) => currentValue.filter((item) => item !== requestId) );
} export const isLoadingOverlayShown = derived( loadingOverlayQueue, (requests) => requests.length > 0
);

Now, all done. We've created an original animated loading indicator in a few easy steps. The source code can be found on my GitHub. Do you enjoy working with svelte?
Items.Author: pavlov

Items.Title: 建立一个创业公司| 第 11 - 15 天 | Building a startup | Days 11 - 15
Items.Description:
阿德维克·古普塔

发表于大家好，

过去 4 天我一直在旅行，但我认为到目前为止，我在添加的功能方面取得了最大的进步。

我终于完成了网站的第一个主要“硬”部分。 添加连接、发送和接受请求等功能。这需要一些时间，但现在已经完成。

这是该站点的一个非常重要的功能，因此将其排除在外是一种很大的解脱。

我没有计划在开发的早期就构建通知，但这是我想做的事情之一，所以我做到了。 现在，我拥有了准备好通知的整个基础架构，并且可以轻松地将其进一步整合到应用程序中。

我还发现一件事我可以在应用程序中做得更好。 但是，我将不得不更改太多东西来解决这个问题，并且我想快速完成 MVP，所以我将它保留在 V2 中。

那东西是👇

目前，我在每个用户上存储 2 个东西，连接和通知，我可以将它们移动到他们自己的集合中，而不是将它们作为数组存储在用户本身上。

所以这将是我希望在应用程序的 V2 中改进的地方。
接下来，我将在应用程序中构建聊天。 一旦解决了这个问题，我们就会得到真正的好东西，或者坏的东西，因为我只能想象建造它需要多少努力和时间。

这当然是游戏本身。 我不知道游戏和房间的功能将如何运作。 我的脑子里有它，但是当你开始编码时它就不同了。 所以当我到达它时，我的手指交叉了🤞
毕竟不可能那么糟糕。 好吧，就目前而言，该应用程序做得很好，进展也很好，而且至少还会持续一段时间。 希望以后也能保持这种状态🤞

直到下次👋

阿德维克

有关应用程序的更多进展，请访问

推特

领英

我为这家初创公司编写代码，并向您展示从头开始制作它的整个过程。 该链接将在我的个人资料中。



原文:

Advik Gupta

Posted on May 26

Hey guys,

I have been travelling these past 4 days, but I think I have made the most progress in terms of features I have added in this much time so far.

1) Connections ✅ 2) App notifications ✅ 3) Desktop notifications ✅ 4) Make the remaining site responsive ✅

I finally finished doing the first major 'hard' part of the site. The functionality to add connections, send and accept requests etc. It took some time, but now it's done.

This is a pretty significant feature of the site, so it's a big relief to get it out of the way.

I hadn't planned to build out notifications this early into development, but it was one of those things where I just felt like making it, so I did. Now I have the whole infrastructure to make notifications ready and can easily keep incorporating this further in the app.

I also found one thing I could have done better in the app. But, I will have to change too many things to fix this, and I want to finish the MVP fast, so I keep it for V2.

That thing is 👇

Currently, I am storing 2 things on every user, Connections and Notifications, that I could move to their own collections instead of storing them as arrays on the user itself.

So this would be something I would love to improve in V2 of the app.
Up next I am going to build the chatting in the app. And once that's out of the way, we'll get to the real good stuff, or bad cause I can only imagine how much effort and time it's gonna take to build this.

This of course is the games themselves. I have no idea how the games and room functionally will work. I have it in my brain, but it's different when you start coding it out. So fingers crossed for when I get to it 🤞
It can't be that bad after all. (don't quote me on that)

Well, for now, the app is doing great, progress is doing great, and it's going to be that way for at least some more time. Hopefully remains that way after as well 🤞

Until next time 👋

Advik

For more progress on the app head on to

Youtube

Twitter

LinkedIn

Instagram

Github


PS - You can also check out the youtube channel that I started recently for videos related to the startup. I code the startup and show you the whole journey of making it from scratch. The link for that will be on my profile.
Items.Author: advikguptadev

Items.Title: 在开发单页网站之前要问的 6 个问题 | 6 Questions to Ask Before Developing a Single Page Website
Items.Description:
为个人或商业目的开发网站从未如此简单。 您必须考虑许多事实并得出可能影响您网站排名和受众数量的决定。 特别是，当您计划开发单页网站时，会更加复杂。 设计和开发可能很简单，但您必须考虑您网站的内容。 在本文中，我们将讨论开发单页网站时开发人员和设计人员可能会想到的问题。

什么时候需要单页网站？

这可能是要问的最重要的问题。 为此，您需要确定您的网站的目的是什么。

  • 如果您正在开发内容最少的个人网站，单页网站是完美的选择。

  • 如果您拥有一家小型企业，例如一家咖啡馆或一家产品很少的艺术画廊。

  • 如果您计划发起一项活动，则该网站只需要一个页面即可。

  • 策划婚礼或仪式等活动需要最少的内容。 为此，单页是完美的。

  • 如果您是自由职业者或尝试创建在线简历，那么单页将非常适合展示您的技能。

嘿，您在寻找CSS 反射吗？ 可用于在网页中显示元素反射的 CSS 属性。

如何设计布局？

一旦你决定了你的网站将服务于什么目的，让我们开始设计吧。 首先，您应该记住，由于只有一页，您几乎没有机会用内容打动观众。

  • 保持设计简单。 专注于主要内容并以逻辑设计对其进行描绘，以便当访问者进入您的网站时，他们会立即找到必要的信息。

  • 该网站应该是响应式的并且跨浏览器兼容。 目前，大多数人使用移动设备访问网站，观众可以通过他们希望使用的任何设备访问您的网站。

  • 添加动态颜色、渐变和字体，使内容在桌面和移动设备上集中且可读。

如何规划网站的内容？

内容是您网站的重要组成部分。 您必须仔细并有逻辑地计划它，以便用户可以从那里获得所有必需的信息。

  • 将页面分成多个部分并为每个部分创建单独的标题。

  • 如果您添加包含部分标题的导航栏，这将是一个很好的用户体验。 用户只需单击导航链接即可移动到他们想要的任何部分。

  • 简单的文本内容可能会变得无聊。 通过在每个内容的断点处添加图像使其变得有趣。

  • 不要忘记在页面底部添加“返回顶部”按钮。 这减轻了用户花费在向上滚动上的时间。 此外，保留一个固定的标题，以便用户可以随时导航到其他内容。

另请查看CSS 重复渐变— repeating-linear-gradient() 函数允许我们将重复的径向或线性渐变定义为 CSS 图像。 这可用于创建旋转时不重复的渐变。

如何使用媒体元素？

如今，人们不仅仅关注文本内容。 为了吸引更多观众，您还需要添加音频和视频元素。

  • 普通观众花费大量时间在互联网上观看视频。 如果您将相关的视频内容添加到您的网站，用户将更加关注您的页面。

  • 计划对与您的网站相关的高质量照片进行一些投资。 为了给网站的观众留下完美的第一印象，反映您网站动机和品牌的产品图片会让观众更倾向于一次又一次地访问您的网站。

  • 许多网站使用带有描述照片描述或网站如何为用户服务的标题的图像轮播。 在您的网站上嵌入少量此类轮播，以使内容更具吸引力。

如何定位浏览器和用户区域？

在早期，IE 是唯一可用于访问网站的浏览器。 但是现在有数百种浏览器，每种浏览器都有自己的设置和呈现网页的方式。 在您启动网站之前，跨浏览器测试非常重要。

  • 修复你的目标区域。 如果您想让您的网站可供本地或普通观众使用，Chrome、Firefox、IE 和 Safari 这 4 大浏览器就足够了。 但是，如果您想提高您网站的全球排名，还需要考虑其他因素。

  • 对特定区域和国家进行一些研究，找出这些区域最常使用的浏览器。 在您的本地计算机或 Lambatest 等云平台上执行跨浏览器兼容性测试，以确定您的网站是否在所有这些平台上都正确呈现。

不要忘记检查CSS revert value - 一个 CSS 关键字值，它将属性的值重置为浏览器在其 UA 样式表中指定的默认值，就好像网页没有包含任何 CSS。

如何使您的网站安全

如果您的网站处理敏感数据，例如从用户那里收集消息和电子邮件详细信息，那么您的网站的安全性应该是重中之重。 执行此操作的最佳方法是通过购买 SSL 证书并启用 HTTPS 来保护您的网站。 在上述事实中，我们只涵盖了在开发单页应用程序之前要考虑的基本事实。 一旦开始开发，您就会发现在实施您的想法之前还有更多需要考虑的事情。 比如内容应该是动态的还是静态的、用户体验方法、选择完美的 SSL 证书等等。 所以快点，开始开发你的网站。 如果您对创建单页网站有任何特殊建议或问题，请发表评论并告诉我们。



原文:

Developing a website, either for personal or for the commercial purpose have never been easy. You must consider many facts and conclude on decisions that may impact your site’s ranking as well as audience count. Especially, when you are planning to develop a single page website, complications arise even more. The design and development may be simple, but you have to think about your site’s content. In this article, we shall discuss the questions that may arise on the developer’s and designer’s mind while developing a single page website.

When Do You Need A Single Page Website?

This is probably the most important question to ask. For this, you need to decide what is the purpose of your website.

  • Single page website is perfect if you are developing your personal website with minimum content.

  • If you own a small business, like a café or an art gallery with very few products.

  • If you are planning to launch a campaign, you do not need more than a single page for the website.

  • Planning an event like a wedding or a ceremony will require minimum content. For that, a single page is perfect.

  • If you are a freelancer or trying to create an online resume, a single page will be perfect to showcase your skillsets.

Hey, are you looking for CSS Reflections? The CSS property that you can use to display a reflection of an element in your web page.

How to Design the Layout?

Once you have decided what purpose your website is going to serve, let’s come to the design. First, you should remember, since there is only one page, you have very little chance to impress the audience with the content.

  • Keep the design simple. Focus on the main content and portray it in a logical design so that when a visitor enters your website, they find the necessary information instantly.

  • The site should be responsive and cross browser compatible. In present days, most people use mobile devices to access a website and the audience can access your site from any device they wish to.

  • Add dynamic color, gradient, and fonts to make the content focused and readable on the desktop as well as mobile devices.

How to Plan the Site’s Content?

Content is an essential part of your site. You must plan it carefully and logically so that the users can get all the required information from there.

  • Divide the page into sections and create individual headings for each section.

  • It will be a good user experience if you add a navigation bar containing the section headers. Users can move to any section they want by just clicking on a nav link.

  • Simple text content may get boring. Make it interesting by adding images at the breakpoint of each content.

  • Do not forget to add the “back to top” button at the bottom of the page. This relieves the user of the time spent on scrolling up. Also, keep a fixed header so that users can navigate to other content whenever they want.

Also check out CSS Repeating Gradients — The repeating-linear-gradient() function allows us to define a repeating radial or linear gradient as a CSS image. This can be used to create gradients that don’t repeat when rotated.

How to Use Media Elements?

Nowadays, people do not focus on text content only. To attract more audience, you will need to add audio and video elements as well.

  • An average viewer spends a lot of time on the internet watching videos. If you add relevant video content to your website, users will be more engaged to your page.

  • Plan some investment on high-quality photos that are relevant to your website. For the perfect first impression on the site’s audience, product images that reflect your site’s motive and brand make the audience more inclined to visit your website again and again.

  • Many websites use image carousels with captions portraying the photo descriptions or how the site can serve the user. Embed few such carousels on your site to make the content more engaging.

How to Target the Browsers and User Zones?

In earlier days IE was the only browser available to access websites. But nowadays there are hundreds of browsers, each with their own settings and own way of rendering web pages. Cross browser testing is very important before you launch your website.

  • Fix your target zone. If you want to make your website available to the local or general audience, the 4 major browsers, Chrome, Firefox, IE and Safari are enough. However, if you want to increase your site’s ranking worldwide, there are other factors to consider.

  • Do some research on specific zones and countries and find out what browsers are used mostly in those zones. Perform cross-browser compatibility tests either on your local machine or on cloud platforms like Lambatest to find out whether your site is rendered correctly in all of them.

Don’t forget to check out CSS revert value- A CSS keyword value that resets a property’s value to the default specified by the browser in its UA stylesheet, as if the webpage had not included any CSS.

How to Make Your Site Secure

If your site deals with sensitive data, like collecting messages and email details from users, the security of your site should be on top priority. The best way to perform this is to secure your website by purchasing an SSL certificate and enable HTTPS. SSL certificates usually make your site secure by creating a safe connection between the server and the client.

In the above-mentioned facts, we only covered the basic facts to think about before developing a single page application. Once you start developing, you will figure out that there is more to think about before implementing your ideas. Like whether the content should be dynamic or static, user experience approach, choosing the perfect SSL certificate and many others. So hurry up, start developing your website. Leave a comment and let us know if you have any special advice or question for creating a single-page website.
Items.Author: arnabroychowdhury

Items.Title: KodaDot Github 存储库和动态的状态 | Status of KodaDot Github repositories & dynamics
Items.Description:
关于 Kodabot所以，任何人脑海中可能会出现的第一个问题是，“Kodabot 是什么？” 答案是：“Kodabot 是一个激进的开源中心，开发人员正在这里制作基于 Kusama 网络的 NFT 市场引擎。”

截至 2022 年 5 月 26 日的存储库状态


最常用的主题是：-

热门语言是：-

  1. 打字稿
  2. 锈
  3. 杰出贡献者是：-

    1. 安德烈格林
    2. 阳窝
    3. 罗利奥
    4. 顶级存储库是：-

      1. 文档
      2. 鲁比克
      3. 斯内克
      4. 包

      问题
      新问题定期出现在 Kodabot Github 存储库中，其中许多是资助的赏金。 这意味着解决这些问题的开发人员可以在加密货币中赚很多钱。 截至 2022 年 5 月 26 日，存储库“nft-gallery”有 545 个问题和 14 个拉取请求。 要解决与代码相关的问题，只需完成解决过程，提出拉取请求并获得奖品！ 奖品范围从 50 美元到 600 美元左右（以加密货币计）。

      例如，nft-gallery 中标题为“如何为 Rubick 制作 NFT 解析器并在 KodaDot 中使用它”的问题是提供 150-300 美元的赏金。


      机器人的讨论和功能
      讨论经常在 github 上进行。 使用 Assign Bot V1，一个人可以自动为自己分配特定的赏金。 要为自己分配赏金，只需转到 Kodabot 的 github 赏金页面并在聊天中写下👋。 分配机器人将为您分配特定的赏金，并有时间限制以完成赏金。 使用支付机器人，开发人员可以在 PR 之间进行支付。

      如果您遇到问题或有疑问，您可以在 github 讨论中提问，有人可能会回答您的问题。


      代码审查协会
      那么，为什么拥有高质量的代码和经过同行评审的代码库很重要呢？ 代码的质量非常重要，因为它确保了可用性、用户友好性、更快的代码执行和代码的可理解性。 确保代码高效和高质量代码的最佳方法是让同行对其进行审查。

      每当您编写一些代码来解决 Kodabot 问题时，您应该请求您在 github 上的 Kodabot 同行进行审查。 一旦您的代码被审查和接受，并且您的拉取请求被合并到 Kodabot 存储库中，赏金就是您的了！


      解释赏金过程的流程图
      现在，让我们看一下 Kodabot 的一些著名存储库。

      分叉：188 星：260 贡献者：75 问题：545 发布：13

      拉比克

      分叉：9 星：3 贡献者：8 问题：32 发布：2

      文档

      分叉：1 星：2 贡献者：3 问题：14 发布：1

      要贡献，请阅读https://github.com/kodadot/nft-gallery/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md上的贡献.md。



原文:

About Kodabot So, the first question that may arise in anyone's mind would be, "What is Kodabot?" The answer would be:- "Kodabot is a radical opensource hub where developers are making NFT marketplace engine based on Kusama network."

Status of Repositories as of May 26th, 2022


Most used topics are:-

Top languages are:-

  1. TypeScript
  2. Rust
  3. Vue
  4. JavaScript
  5. Python

Top Contributors are:-

  1. AndreyGrin
  2. Yangwao
  3. RoiLeo
  4. Vikiival and many more....

Top Repositories are:-

  1. nft-gallery
  2. docs
  3. rubick
  4. snek
  5. packages

How Kodabot Operations
Issues
New issues arrive regularly at Kodabot Github Repositories and many of them are funded bounties. This means developers who solve those issues can make a lot of money in cryptocurrency. As of May 26th, 2022, the repository 'nft-gallery' has 545 issues and 14 pull requests. To solve a code-related issue, just go through the solution process, make the pull request and have the prize! Prizes range from 50 usd to around 600 usd(in cryptocurrency).

For example, the issue in nft-gallery titled 'how to make NFT resolver to Rubick and use it in KodaDot' is a bounty offering 150-300 usd.


Discussions and functioning of bots
Discussions are often held on github. Kodabot has developed github bots for assignment of bounty as well as bounty distribution. With Assign Bot V1, a person can autoassign himself a specific bounty. To assign yourself the bounty, just go to Kodabot's github bounty page and write 👋 in chat. The Assign Bot will assign you that specific bounty with a time limit to complete the bounty. With the payout bot, developers do payouts between themselves for PRs.

If you are facing some problem or are having some question, you can ask in github discussions and someone would probably answer your query.


Code Review Guild
So, why is it important to have quality code and peer-reviewed codebase? The quality of code is very important because it ensures usability, user-friendliness, faster execution of code and understandability of code. A 'bad' code is not much usable, users don't get a satisfiable experience and code takes a lot of effort to be understood and program takes a lot of time to execute. The best way to ensure that code is efficient and its a good quality code is to have it reviewed by peers.

Whenever you write some code to solve Kodabot issues, you should request your Kodabot peers on github to review it. Once your code is reviewed and accepted and your pull request is merged into the Kodabot repository, the bounty prize is yours!


Flowchart to explain the bounty process
Now, let's take a look at some famous repositories of Kodabot.

NFT Gallery Repository

Forks: 188 Stars: 260 COntributors: 75 Issues: 545 Releases: 13

Rubick

Forks: 9 Stars: 3 Contributors: 8 Issues: 32 Releases: 2

Docs

Forks: 1 Stars: 2 Contributors: 3 Issues: 14 Releases: 1

To contribute, please read contributing.md at https://github.com/kodadot/nft-gallery/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md.
Items.Author: ayushkumar63123

Items.Title: 作为开发人员提高工作效率的 5 个技巧 | 5 Tips to be productive as a Developer
Items.Description:
首先也是最好的事情，尽量减少分心。 社交媒体是结交朋友和闲聊的绝佳平台，但在某些情况下也是最糟糕的。 你可以做这几件事来帮助你把注意力集中在问题上——练习冥想，按照适当的时间表工作，最后变得富有成效而不是忙碌。

保持生产力而不是忙碌 - Tim Ferriss

键入代码可能是一项令人沮丧的任务，并且很容易破坏您的工作效率。 为此，您当然可以利用代码生成，或编写脚本来自动完成需要时间来完成的小型任务。

您要么是创建自动化的人，要么正在实现自动化。 从事新项目可能是保持头脑清醒的好方法。 爱好项目可以更多地反映你自己的想法，并帮助你成长为一名程序员。 附带项目可以帮助您一路学习新技能，进而帮助您提高在未来项目和工作中的生产力。

一旦你停止学习，你就会开始死亡。 大多数成功人士和创业者都坚持写目标和成就，你还在等什么？ 从尽可能多地写作开始，最后你会找到你的道路。
并衡量你的进步并努力解决你的弱点。

成年人制定一个计划并遵循它。 孩子们做感觉良好的事情。 阅读对你有好处，因为它可以提高你的注意力、记忆力、同理心和沟通技巧。 它可以减轻压力，改善您的心理健康，并帮助您延长寿命。 除了开发人员之间的知识转移之外，阅读技术书籍是我们认为提高技能的好方法。 虽然有些书不会告诉你如何处理一个特定的问题，但它会解释你的起源并给你其他的观点。

一本书是你可以一次又一次打开的礼物。 - Garrison Keillor - 加里森·凯勒



原文:

The First and the Best thing, Minimize Distractions. Social media is a great platform for making friends and chit-chatting but is also the worst in some cases. You can do these few things that help you make the best focus on problems - Practice Meditation, Work on a proper schedule, and lastly be productive instead of busy.

Be Productive instead of Busy - Tim Ferriss

2. Take advantage of Automation

Typing code can be a discouraging task and can easily break your productivity. For this, you can certainly take advantage of code generation, or write scripts to small automate tasks that need time to complete.

You’re either the one that creates the automation or you’re getting automated. - Tom Preston-Werner

3. Work on Side Projects and Learn New Skills

Working on new projects can be an excellent way to keep your mind fresh. Hobby projects can reflect more on your own ideas and help you grow as a programmer. The side projects can help you learn new skills along the way, which in turn will help you improve your productivity in future projects and works.

Once you stop learning, you start dying. - Albert Einstein

4. Stay Disciplined

Most of the successful people and the entrepreneurs insist on writing goals and achievements, so what are you waiting for? start by writing as much as you can and finally you will find your path.
And also Measure your progress and work on your weaknesses.

Adults devise a plan and follow it. Children do what feels good. - Dave Ramsey

5. Read Books and Level up your skills

Reading is good for you because it improves your focus, memory, empathy, and communication skills. It can reduce stress, improve your mental health, and help you live longer. Aside from knowledge transfer between developers, reading the tech books is what we consider a good way to improve our skills. While some of the books will not tell you how to deal with a particular problem, it will explain you the origin and give you other perspectives.

A book is a gift you can open again and again. - Garrison Keillor

Items.Title: AwardWallet 的行程规划器已成为 Uber Travel 体验的一部分 | AwardWallet’s itinerary planner has become part of Uber Travel experience
Items.Description:
优步旅行允许用户通过将他们即将到来的旅行和预订与优步乘车联系起来来创建路线。 借助 AwardWallet 行程规划器和 Uber 乘车服务，用户无需付出任何努力即可自动完成此操作。 您唯一需要做的就是使用 Gmail 帐户登录，以便该应用程序可以接收有关预订的信息。 这是安全的，因为该应用程序只会跟踪与旅行相关的电子邮件。

结果，用户获得了顺畅的旅行体验，因为他们的旅程从开始到结束都是计划好的。 此外，他们会收到有关途中任何延误的通知，并可以相应地更改他们的计划。 用户可以提前 30 天预订游乐设施，这有助于规划预算。 每次通过 Uber Travel 预订的行程都会获得 10% 的 Uber Cash 奖励。

关于优步旅行

关于AwardWallet API



原文:

In May Uber announced the introduction of a new feature to their app called Uber Travel (along with other new items, such as Voice Ordering, Uber Charter, Uber Eats at Stadiums, etc.)

Uber Travel allows users to create a route by linking their upcoming trips and reservations with Uber rides. This happens automatically without any effort on users’ part thanks to AwardWallet itinerary planner and Uber car rides. The only thing you have to do is to sign in with Gmail account so that the app could receive information about the bookings. It is safe, because the app will only track travel related emails.

As a result, users get a smooth travel experience, because their journey is planned from start till finish. Moreover, they get notifications about any delays on the way and can alter their plans accordingly. Users can reserve the rides 30 days in advance and that helps with planning a budget. A bonus of 10% Uber Cash is given on each ride booked through Uber Travel.

Uber’s CEO, Dara Khosrowshahi, believes that ‘people have been sitting around at home for too long’ and that with Uber Travel ‘you can rest assured that you’re going to make it to the flight on time’.

About Uber Travel

About AwardWallet API
Items.Author: sergikulakov

Items.Title: 了解 useEvent() React hook 的必要性。 | Understanding the need for useEvent() React hook.
Items.Description:
大家好，

在这篇文章中，让我们尝试了解useEvent React 钩子的需求，我们将看到 React 团队试图用这个钩子解决什么问题。

注意： useEvent还不是 React 的一部分，它只是一个解决不必要的重新渲染问题的建议。

如果您更喜欢这篇文章的视频版本，那么这里是：

让我们通过两个例子来理解 RFC。

示例一

import { useState , useCallback } from " react " ; export default function App () { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( 0 ); const incrementCount = useCallback (() => { setCount ( count + 1 ); }, [ count ]); return ( < div className = " App " > < span > { count } < /span > < button onClick = { incrementCount } > SUBSCRIBE < /button > < /div > ); }

上面的代码看起来很完美，它是一个相当简单的计数器组件。 但问题在于incrementCount处理程序。

每当计数更改时，组件就会重新渲染，并且在每次渲染时都会从头开始重新创建incrementCount处理程序。

好吧，对于这么小的示例，这不会是一个问题，但是在更大的应用程序中，这可能会产生性能和优化问题。

即使我们使用了useCallback钩子，但是因为useCallback在依赖数组中计数，所以问题仍然存在。 在每次计数更改时， useCallback都会再次运行。

解决方案：使用Event来救援。

没有依赖数组： useEvent将没有依赖数组，因此不会在每次状态更改时重新渲染。

  • 访问更新状态：由于闭包， useEvent将始终可以访问最新状态。

    • 一旦useEvent可供使用，该解决方案将类似于以下代码。

     import { useState , useEvent } from " react " ; export default function App () { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( 0 ); const incrementCount = useEvent (() => { setCount ( count + 1 ); }); return ( < div className = " App " > < span > { count } < /span > < button onClick = { incrementCount } > SUBSCRIBE < /button > < /div > ); }

    示例二

    import { useState , useEffect } from " react " ; export default function App () { const [ routeUrl , setRouteUrl ] = useState ( " /home " ); const [ userName , setUserName ] = useState ( " Swastik " ); const logAnalytics = ( routeUrl , userName ) => { console . log ( `Route changed by ${ userName } to ${ routeUrl } ` ); }; useEffect (() => { logAnalytics ( routeUrl , userName ); }, [ logAnalytics , routeUrl , userName ]); return ( < div className = " App " > < /div > ); }

    在上面的示例中，每当routeUrl或userName更改时，都会对分析进行控制。 但我们不希望这样，我们只希望在routeUrl更改而不是userName时对分析进行安慰。

    当用户名更改时，您为什么要记录分析？

    由于依赖数组，useCallback 再次无法解决该问题。 我们需要一些没有依赖数组并且始终可以访问更新状态的东西。

    好吧，似乎一个新的钩子即将加入必不可少的 React 钩子列表。

    解决方案：再次使用Event来救援。

    一旦 useEvent 结束，解决方案将类似于以下代码。

     import { useState , useEffect , useEvent } from " react " ; export default function App () { const [ routeUrl , setRouteUrl ] = useState ( " /home " ); const [ userName , setUserName ] = useState ( " Swastik " ); const logAnalytics = useEvent (( routeUrl ) => { console . log ( `Route changed by ${ userName } to ${ routeUrl } ` ); }); useEffect (() => { logAnalytics ( routeUrl ); }, [ logAnalytics , routeUrl ]); return ( < div className = " App " > < /div > ); }

    我希望你喜欢这篇文章。 如果是这样，请通过下面的评论参与讨论。

    我还运行每周时事通讯，最近还开设了一个youtube频道。

    请表示您的支持

    谢谢你！



    原文:

    Hello Everyone,

    In this post, let's try to understand the need of useEvent React hook and we will see what problem the React team is trying to solve with this hook.

    Note: useEvent is yet not part of React, it is just a proposal to solve the problem of unnecessary rerendering.

    If you prefer a video version of this post then here it is:

    2 weeks ago an RFC was proposed by React team in which they introduced an new React hook called the useEvent hook.

    Let's understand that RFC with two examples.

    Example One 

    import { useState, useCallback } from "react"; export default function App() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); const incrementCount = useCallback(() => { setCount(count + 1); }, [count]); return ( <div className="App"> <span>{count}</span>
 <button onClick={incrementCount}>SUBSCRIBE</button>
 </div>
 );
}

    The above code looks perfectly fine, It is a fairly simple counter component. But the problem is with incrementCount handler.

    Whenever the count changes the component rerenders, and on every render the incrementCount handler is created again from scratch.

    Well this will not be an issue with such a small example, but in larger applications this may create an issue with performance and optimization.

    Even though we have used the useCallback hook, but because useCallback takes count in dependency array the problem remains the same. On every count change useCallback will run again.

    Solution: useEvent to the rescue.

    useEvent will solve the above problem in two ways:

    • No dependency array: useEvent will have no dependency array, hence no rerendering on every state change.
    • Access to the updated states: useEvent will always have access to the latest states due to closures.

    Once useEvent is available to use, the solution will look something similar to the below code.

    import { useState, useEvent } from "react"; export default function App() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); const incrementCount = useEvent(() => { setCount(count + 1); }); return ( <div className="App"> <span>{count}</span>
 <button onClick={incrementCount}>SUBSCRIBE</button>
 </div>
 );
}

    Example Two 

    import { useState, useEffect } from "react"; export default function App() { const [routeUrl, setRouteUrl] = useState("/home"); const [userName, setUserName] = useState("Swastik"); const logAnalytics = (routeUrl, userName) => { console.log(`Route changed by ${userName} to ${routeUrl}`); }; useEffect(() => { logAnalytics(routeUrl, userName); }, [logAnalytics, routeUrl, userName]); return ( <div className="App"> </div>
 );
}

    In the above example analytics is consoled whenever routeUrl or userName changes. But we don't want that, we only want analytics to be consoled when routeUrl changes not userName.

    Why would you log analytics when userName changes?

    useCallback again won't solve that problem because of dependency array. We need something that does not have dependency array and always have access to the updated states.

    Well, it seems that a new hook is about to join the list of essential React hooks.

    Solution: useEvent to the rescue again.

    The solution will look similar to the below code once useEvent is out.

    import { useState, useEffect, useEvent } from "react"; export default function App() { const [routeUrl, setRouteUrl] = useState("/home"); const [userName, setUserName] = useState("Swastik"); const logAnalytics = useEvent((routeUrl) => { console.log(`Route changed by ${userName} to ${routeUrl}`); }); useEffect(() => { logAnalytics(routeUrl); }, [logAnalytics, routeUrl]); return ( <div className="App"> </div>
 );
}

    I hope you enjoyed this post. If so, do participate in the discussion by commenting below.

    I also run a weekly newsletter and very recently started a youtube channel.

    Please show your support

    Thank You!
Items.Author: swastikyadav

Items.Title: 在智能手机上托管 TOR 隐藏服务（DARK WEB 网站）！ | Host TOR Hidden service (DARK WEB website) on a smartphone !!
Items.Description:
山姆赛酚

发表于在这篇博客中，我们将在 Android 中使用 termux 托管一个 TOR 隐藏服务。 这太酷了，只需几分钟即可设置……你必须试试这个！！

如果您喜欢视频教程，请单击此处

如果您不想学习该过程，我有我的脚本为您完成工作。 在这里检查 - https://github.com/sam5epi0l/onionX/

按着这些次序 -

  • 安装依赖项

    pkg i termux-api tor git -y


  • 创建torrc配置

    • cp /data/data/com.termux/files/usr/etc/tor/torrc . echo "HiddenServiceDir /data/data/com.termux/files/home/hidden_service/" >> torrc

    回声“隐藏服务端口 80 127.0.0.1:1337”>> torrc


    • 启动TOR隐藏服务

      tor -f torrc


    • 检查主机名文件中的 URL

      cat /data/data/com.termux/files/home/hidden_service/hostname


    • 我在 127.0.0.1:1337 使用 python http 服务器

      python3 -m http.server 1337

      欢呼！ 您完成了所有步骤，主机已启动并运行 DARK WEB 网站。 非常感谢您的阅读。



    原文:

    sam-sepiol

    Posted on May 26

    DARK WEB websites — TOR hidden services or the .onion sites which can only be accessed by connecting to TOR relay.

    In this blog, We will host a TOR hidden service using termux in Android. This is incredibly cool, takes only a few minutes to setup…you’ve gotta try this!!

    If you prefer a video tutorial, click here

    If you don’t want to learn the process, I have my script to do the work for you. Check it here — https://github.com/sam5epi0l/onionX/

    Follow these steps -

  • Install dependencies

    pkg i termux-api tor git -y


  • create torrc configuration

    • cp /data/data/com.termux/files/usr/etc/tor/torrc . echo "HiddenServiceDir /data/data/com.termux/files/home/hidden_service/" >> torrc

    echo "HiddenServicePort 80 127.0.0.1:1337" >> torrc


    • Start TOR hidden service

      tor -f torrc


    • Check URL in hostname file

      cat /data/data/com.termux/files/home/hidden_service/hostname


    • (OPTIONAL) Start your Apache/nginx web server at port 1337

    I’m using python http server at 127.0.0.1:1337

    python3 -m http.server 1337

    Hurray! you finished all steps and the host is up and running DARK WEB website. Thank you so much for reading.
Items.Author: sam5epi0l

Items.Title: 带有 Next.js 路由的活动导航栏 | Active Navbar with Next.js Routes
Items.Description:
主动导航栏意味着当用户在about页面上时， about链接应该在导航栏中突出显示。 当用户在不同的页面上并且其链接在导航栏中可用时，应该突出显示。

如果您不明白，那么以下是我正在谈论的内容的演示以及结果如何-

预习

正如您在上面的演示中看到的，当用户单击任何导航栏部分时，页面路径会更改，并且活动页面名称会在导航栏上突出显示。 这就是我们要建造的。

创建布局

如果您没有布局，则需要先创建布局。 并将该Layout组件添加到_app.js 。

 // components/Layout.jsx import Navbar from " ./Navbar " ; export default function Layout ({ children }) { return ( <> < Navbar /> { children } { /* Footer */ } { /* You can add more things here */ } </> ); }

在上面的代码中，您可以看到我们正在导入Navbar ，然后我们在Layout中渲染Navbar 。

现在，在创建 Layout 之后，我们需要用 Layout 来包装我们的整个应用程序。 它应该看起来像这样-

 // pages/_app.js import " ../styles/global.css " ; import Layout from " ./../components/Layout " ; function MyApp ({ Component , pageProps }) { return ( < Layout > < Component { ... pageProps } /> </ Layout > ); } export default MyApp ;

创建导航栏

在 components 文件夹中创建文件名Navbar.jsx 。 首先，导入Link和useRouter ，因为我们需要它们来检查当前页面路由。

 // components/Navbar.jsx import { useRouter } from " next/router " ; import Link from " next/link " ;

然后我们需要定义我们拥有并希望在导航栏上显示的所有路线。

 // components/Navbar.jsx export default function Navbar () { const router = useRouter (); return ( < nav className = "nav_container" > { navigationRoutes . map (( singleRoute ) => { return ( < NavigationLink key = { singleRoute } href = { `/ ${ singleRoute } ` } text = { singleRoute } router = { router } /> ); }) } </ nav > ); }

在上面的代码中，我们定义了router ，然后我们创建了一个nav容器。 之后，我正在映射navigationRoutes ，对于每条路线，我们将返回NavigationLink ，我们将在一分钟内创建它。
完整的导航代码-

 import { useRouter } from " next/router " ; import Link from " next/link " ; const navigationRoutes = [ " home " , " about " , " pricing " , " contact " ]; export default function Navbar () { const router = useRouter (); return ( < nav className = "nav_container" > { navigationRoutes . map (( singleRoute ) => { return ( < NavigationLink key = { singleRoute } href = { `/ ${ singleRoute } ` } text = { singleRoute } router = { router } /> ); }) } </ nav > ); } function NavigationLink ({ href , text , router }) { const isActive = router . asPath === ( href === " /home " ? " / " : href ); return ( < Link href = { href === " /home " ? " / " : href } passHref > < a href = { href === " /home " ? " / " : href } className = { ` ${ isActive && " nav_item_active " } nav_item` } > < span > { text } </ span > </ a > </ Link > ); }

在此，我们检查当前路由路径是否与href相同，然后为isActive返回true ，如果当前路由处于活动状态，则应用nav_item_active类。

这就是我们创建活动导航栏所需的全部内容，并且可以完美运行。 您可以在以下沙箱上查看现场演示。

包起来

如果您喜欢这篇文章，请不要忘记按❤️并将其添加为书签以备后用。 如果您有任何疑问或建议，请不要犹豫放弃它们。 再见。

您可以通过给我买咖啡来扩大您的支持。😊👇

我的时事通讯



原文:

Active Navbar means that when the user is on the about page, the about link should be highlighted in the navbar. and when the user is on a different page and its link is available in the navbar, that should be highlighted.

If you don't get it then the following is the demo of what I am talking about and how it's gonna turn out-

Preview

As you can see in the above demo when the user clicks any of the navbar sections the page route changes and the active page name is highlighted on the navbar. That's what are we going to build.

Creating Layout

If you don't have a Layout then you need to create a layout first. and Add that Layout component to the _app.js.

// components/Layout.jsx import Navbar from "./Navbar"; export default function Layout({ children }) { return ( <> <Navbar /> {children} {/* Footer */} {/* You can add more things here */} </> );
}

In the above code, you can see that we are importing Navbar and then we are rendering the Navbar inside the Layout.

Now, after creating Layout we need to wrap our whole app with Layout. It should look something like this-

// pages/_app.js import "../styles/global.css";
import Layout from "./../components/Layout"; function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }) { return ( <Layout> <Component {...pageProps} /> </Layout> );
} export default MyApp;

Creating Navbar

Create a file name Navbar.jsx in the components folder. and first, import the Link and useRouter as we need those to check the current page route.

// components/Navbar.jsx import { useRouter } from "next/router";
import Link from "next/link";

Then we need to define all the routes which we have and want to show on the navbar.

// components/Navbar.jsx export default function Navbar() { const router = useRouter(); return ( <nav className="nav_container"> {navigationRoutes.map((singleRoute) => { return ( <NavigationLink key={singleRoute} href={`/${singleRoute}`} text={singleRoute} router={router} /> ); })} </nav> );
}

In the above code, we are defining the router and then we are creating a nav container. After that, I am mapping navigationRoutes and for each route, we are returning NavigationLink which we will create in a minute.
*Props: *

  • href: route link
  • text: text that will be displayed on the navigation bar
  • router: verify the current route 
// components/Navbar.jsx function NavigationLink({ href, text, router }) { const isActive = router.asPath === (href === "/home" ? "/" : href); return ( <Link href={href === "/home" ? "/" : href} passHref> <a href={href === "/home" ? "/" : href} className={`${isActive && "nav_item_active"} nav_item`}> {text} </a> </Link> );
}

Full Navigation Code- 

import { useRouter } from "next/router";
import Link from "next/link"; const navigationRoutes = ["home", "about", "pricing", "contact"]; export default function Navbar() { const router = useRouter(); return ( <nav className="nav_container"> {navigationRoutes.map((singleRoute) => { return ( <NavigationLink key={singleRoute} href={`/${singleRoute}`} text={singleRoute} router={router} /> ); })} </nav> );
} function NavigationLink({ href, text, router }) { const isActive = router.asPath === (href === "/home" ? "/" : href); return ( <Link href={href === "/home" ? "/" : href} passHref> <a href={href === "/home" ? "/" : href} className={`${isActive && "nav_item_active"} nav_item`}> <span>{text}</span> </a> </Link> );
}

In this, we check if the current router path is the same as the href then return true for isActive and if the current route is active then apply the nav_item_active class.

This is all we need to create an active navigation bar and it works flawlessly. You can check the live demo on the following sandbox.

Wrapping Up

If you enjoyed this article then don't forget to press ❤️ and Bookmark it for later use. If you have any queries or suggestions don't hesitate to drop them. See you.

You can extend your support by buying me a Coffee.😊👇

My Newsletter

Items.Title: 了解您拥有良好开发人员体验的 4 种方法 | 4 Ways to Know You Have Good Developer Experience
Items.Description:
由于开发人员总是与代码、软件和人类进行交互，因此可以合理地假设他们对这些交互有感觉。 感情可能是好的，也可能是坏的。 开发人员体验是开发人员对在日常编码活动中与之交互的代码、软件和人类的感受的本质。

当开发人员使用能够提高他们工作效率并让他们对自己的组织和角色感到满意的软件和平台时，他们正在体验积极的开发人员体验。 但是，如果他们对组织的平台感到疲倦和沮丧，并且觉得他们花费更多时间获取权限和数据而不是实际创建，那么他们就会有负面的开发人员体验。 所以，问题是你怎么知道你有良好的开发经验？

软件开发人员必须使用多种软件来执行他们的日常编程活动。 他们使用内部代码库以及外部代码库。 他们使用这些工具的经验在很大程度上决定了他们的生产力和经验，最终影响到他们的公司。

如果某个代码库或工具易于使用且直观，那么开发人员肯定会有很好的体验，因为它在提供高生产力的同时花费相对较少的精力。 如果软件有缺陷、缺乏适当的文档或设计时没有考虑到最终用户的利益，则它们可能很难使用。 如果所有这些规定都为您提供了充分的条件，则表明您作为开发人员拥有良好的经验。

开发人员门户是有助于在其他领域中轻松使用的平台，因为它们为开发人员提供了获取所需数据或权限的自助服务机会，而无需跳过多个环节来寻找合适的人。

开发人员不喜欢一遍又一遍地做一件事，这在为避免一直以相同方式做某事而构建的工具数量中很明显。 这种情况很容易使开发人员筋疲力尽。 将大多数单调的任务自动化，让大多数开发人员非常满意。

例如，如果开发人员必须使用错误跟踪器来自动发现错误，而不是手动跟踪堆栈跟踪，他们会有很好的体验。 如果单调乏味的任务被自动化，这表明开发人员拥有良好的体验。 这也适用于开发人员与组织中其他团队的交互——任何可以自动化交互和权限的方式都将增强开发人员的体验。

一个能够促进和支持开发人员福祉、成长、生产力并鼓励信任的工作环境表明您拥有良好的开发人员体验。 这不仅仅是技术支持和指导； 它主要是为开发人员提供一个有利的环境。 这意味着让他们能够顺利工作并获取信息，而无需额外的障碍或与多个团队中的人员进行不必要的来回交流。 一个好的工作环境是允许开发人员无缝地完成他们的工作并通过它感觉得到很好的支持。

有必要选择一种架构，以确保开发人员在没有不必要的挫折的情况下启动和运行。 采取此措施可确保实现业务目标，同时开发人员也拥有丰富的经验。

使用复杂或不合适的架构会使开发人员感到沮丧并推动他们，从而造成糟糕的开发人员体验。 因此，拥有合适的架构表明良好的开发人员体验。 从构建应用程序的平台的角度考虑开发人员的架构——他们需要工具、库、指南和文档。

最终，良好的开发人员体验架构看起来像是一个授权环境。 松散耦合的架构为开发人员提供了更多的自主权来构建和做出决策。

良好的开发人员体验的重要性

拥有良好的开发人员体验对企业、开发人员及其社区很重要的原因有很多。 其中一些原因是：

业务增长

对于一些以开发人员为中心的企业，有必要为他们所服务的开发人员提供良好的体验，因为这样的开发人员可以影响他们公司的决策。 例如，在使用付费API 服务器时遇到很多摩擦的开发人员可以影响他们的公司光顾另一个 API 服务器，从而为他们提供更好的体验。 在这种情况下，开发人员体验糟糕的企业会失去客户和收入。

为客户带来丰富的体验

良好的开发人员体验的一个重要好处是它激励开发人员培养使命感。 他们的工作不仅是为了获得报酬，也是为了服务最终用户。 这种发展反过来又确保了用户拥有良好的体验，这对业务有利。

创新

由于开发人员愿意为最终用户服务，他们往往会努力工作和创新以保持领先于竞争对手。 这种态度可以带来更多的赞助，从而带来收入和业务增长。 投入时间构建积极的开发人员体验环境的最佳部分意味着创新不会被复杂的工作流程所扼杀。 结论

如果没有正确定义“良好的开发人员体验”，则很难确定您的开发人员体验是好还是坏。 对人类来说，好坏是相对的，因为背景、取向、信仰和接触的不同。 甚至开发人员在某个时间点的心情和技能也可能决定他们的体验是好是坏。 那么，什么是好的开发者体验？

当开发人员对与他们一起工作的代码、软件和人类的交互感觉相对良好时，就可以获得良好的开发人员体验——并且可以控制挫折。 考虑到这一点，您可以客观地确定您是否具有良好的开发人员经验。 无论如何，这篇文章中指出的四种方法对于确定您是否拥有良好的开发人员体验很有用。



原文:


(photo by Christina @ wocintechchat.com

As developers are always interacting with code, software, and human beings, it is reasonable to assume they have feelings about these interactions. The feelings may be good or bad. Developer experience is the nature of developers’ feelings towards code, software, and human beings they interact with in their day to day coding activities.

When developers work with software and platforms that make them productive and give them a sense of satisfaction with their organization and role, they’re experiencing a positive developer experience. However, if they’re fatigued and frustrated with the organization’s platforms and feel like they spend more time obtaining permissions and data than actually creating, then they have a negative developer experience. So, the question is how do you know you have a good developer experience?

1. Ease of Use

Software developers have to use several software to carry out their day-to-day programming activities. They work with internal codebases as well as external ones. The experience they have in using these tools largely determines their productivity and experience, which eventually affect their companies.

If a certain codebase or tool is easy and intuitive to use, then developers will definitely have a good experience because it will take relatively little effort while providing great productivity. Software can be hard to use if they are buggy, lack proper documentation or are not designed with end users’ interests in mind. If all of these provisions are adequately provided for you, it is a sign you have a good experience as a developer.

Developer portals are platforms that can help ease of use in other domains as they provide self-service opportunities for developers to get the data or permissions they need without having to jump through multiple hoops to find the right person.

2. Reduced Exhaustion

Developers don’t like to do one thing over and over again, and that is evident in the number of tools that have been built to avoid doing something the same way all the time. Such a situation wears out developers easily. Having most monotonous tasks automated gives most developers great satisfaction.

For example, a developer has a good experience if they have to use an error-tracker to automatically spot errors instead of tracing the stack trace manually. It is a sign a developer is having a good experience if monotonous and exhausting tasks are automated. This applies to the developer’s interactions with other teams in the organization as well – any way that can automate interactions and permissions will augment developer experience.

3. Great Working Environment

A work environment that enables and supports developers wellbeing, growth, productivity and encourages trust shows that you have a good developer experience. This goes beyond just technical support and mentorship; it is mostly about providing an enabling environment for developers. This means giving them the ability to work smoothly and obtain information without extra hurdles or unnecessary back-and-forth with people in multiple teams. A great working environment is one that allows a developer to do their work seamlessly and feel well-supported through it.

4. Suitable Architecture


(source)

It is necessary to choose an architecture that ensures developers get up and running without unnecessary frustration. Taking this measure makes sure business goals are achieved while developers also have a great experience.

Working with a complex or unsuitable architecture frustrates developers and pushes them, creating a bad developer experience. Therefore, having a fitting architecture is an indication of a good developer experience. Think about architecture for developers in terms of a platform for building applications – they expect tools, libraries, guides, and documentation.

Ultimately, a good developer experience architecture looks like an empowering environment. Loosely coupled architecture gives developers more autonomy to build and make decisions.

Importance of a Good Developer Experience

There are so many reasons why having a good developer experience is important for businesses, developers, and their communities. Some of the reasons are:

Business Growth

For some developer-focused businesses, it is necessary to provide a great experience for the developers they serve because such developers can influence the decisions of their companies. For example, a developer who experiences a lot of friction while consuming a paid API server, can influence their companies to patronize another API server that gives them a better experience. In this case, the business with a terrible developer experience loses clients and revenue.

Great Experience for the Customer

One important benefit of a good developer experience is that it incentivizes developers to develop a sense of mission. They tend to work not just to get paid but also to serve the end user. This development in turn ensures users have a good experience, which is good for business.

Innovation

As developers are willing to serve the end user, they tend to work hard and innovate to stay ahead of their competitors. Such an attitude can lead to more patronage and, consequently, revenue and business growth. The best part about dedicating time to building a positive developer experience environment means that innovation will not be stifled by complex workflows. Innovation in DevOps pipelines can continue as is and the developers will be motivated by it.

Conclusion

It is hard or misleading to determine whether you are having a good or bad developer experience if “good developer experience” is not properly defined. To human beings, good or bad is relative because of differences in backgrounds, orientations, beliefs and exposures. Even developers’ moods and skills at a point in time may determine whether they have a good or bad experience. So, what is good developer experience?

Good developer experience is achieved when developers feel relatively good about their interactions with the codes, software, and humans they work with—with manageable frustration. With that in mind, you can objectively determine whether you have good developer experience or not. Anyway, the four ways pointed out in this write-up are useful for determining whether you have a good developer experience or not.

Items.Title: 新拟态金字塔 | Neumorphism Pyramid
Items.Description:
理查德·伦伯特

发表于新拟态意味着对眼睛柔和。 它要求最小的颜色对比度和很少的颜色流行。 从逻辑上讲，设计师可以自由地以他们认为合适的任何程度应用新拟态——不必完全实现这种风格。

这个金字塔是在考虑到神经拟态的基础上构建的，并在 javascript 的帮助下，随着光标移动。

Suraj Vishwakarma - Suraj Vishwakarma - May 12 5月12日



原文:

Richard Rembert

Posted on May 26

Neumorphism is meant to be soft on the eyes. It calls for minimal color contrast and very few pops of color. Logically, designers are free to apply neumorphism in any degree they see fit – not having to implement the style in its entirety.

This pyramid is built with neumorphism in mind and with the help of javascript, move round with the cursor.

Suraj Vishwakarma - May 12
